calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-17-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-24-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Set For Trial On Oct-17-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. JOHN A. LOFTON, ESQ. and JOHN D. SORENSON, ESQ. of Leland Parachini Steinberg Matzger & Melnick LLP, Tel No. 415-957-1800 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOHN F. PRENTICE, ESQ. of Law Office of John F. Prentice, Tel No. 925-941-6222 appearing for the Defendant Richard Lopez.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RICHARD LOPEZ' Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff's claims are not preempted by ERISA because they allege a 10-year failure to create an IRA as promised, and expressly disclaim any relief relating to an IRA that was later created; defendant cites no case finding preemption in this scenario. As to wrongful termination, no authority is cited for the notion that a complaint to the Labor Commissioner or a third party is required. The remainder of plaintiff's claims are properly pled. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. JOHN A. LOFTON, ESQ. and JOHN D. SORENSON, ESQ. of Leland Parachini Steinberg Matzger & Melnick LLP, Tel No. 415-957-1800 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOHN F. PRENTICE, ESQ. of Law Office of John F. Prentice, Tel No. 925-941-6222 appearing for the Defendant Richard Lopez.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RICHARD LOPEZ' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff's claims are not preempted by ERISA because they allege a 10-year failure to create an IRA as promised, and expressly disclaim any relief relating to an IRA that was later created; defendant cites no case finding preemption in this scenario. As to wrongful termination, no authority is cited for the notion that a complaint to the Labor Commissioner or a third party is required. The remainder of plaintiff's claims are properly pled. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/RBU)
